Hey Product Hunt, I'm Terence, founder of Osaurus. I spent 20 years shipping software at Netflix, Tesla, and Zillow before going all in on this.  
  
Own your AI. Osaurus is an open source AI agent platform that runs on your Mac.

MIT licensed, native Swift for Apple Silicon. No account, no subscription, no Electron.  
  
We started as Dinoki, a 5MB desktop dinosaur, and grew into a full agent platform through open source community feedback. Now at 7K+ GitHub stars and 175K+ downloads — all by word of mouth.

📌 [...] Osaurus keeps all of it on your Mac.  
  
What that means in practice:

 Agents read your Calendar, look up Contacts, send iMessages, and work with your real files
 An isolated sandbox writes and runs code, producing real files on your desktop: images, PDFs, presentations
 Every action goes through an approval gate. You see what the agent wants to do before it does it
 Local models run fully offline. We built our own Swift MLX runtime, no Python underneath
 Want a frontier model? Bring your own key (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini) or use pay-as-you-go Osaurus Cloud. Either way, your memory and context stay local

It's free and always will be.

# Your Mac. Your AI. Free.

Open models run entirely on your Mac — your data never leaves. Add ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini when a task needs them, and carry one shared memory across all of them.

Drop a folder. Give it a job. Osaurus can read, write, and execute — like having a local AI assistant that actually knows your environment.

Osaurus dinos working

Build agents that run autonomously — voice control, folder watchers, browser plugins, parallel jobs. Let your local AI work while you sleep.

Wi-Fi off. Still works. Your data stays on your Mac. Nothing leaves unless you say so — not even anonymous analytics.

Open source

## Open. Forever free. [...] What is local AI?

## Your AI runs here. Not out there.

Most AI tools send everything you type to a server. Local AI keeps the model on your machine — so your conversations, code, and files stay completely private.

As seen onTechCrunch

Osaurus dino at a desk

Private

Nothing leaves your Mac. No telemetry, no training on your data, no servers in the loop.

Offline

Wi-Fi off. Still works. Models run on your Apple Silicon chip — no internet required.

Fast

Inference runs on your M-series chip. No round trips to a server — results in milliseconds.

Free

MIT licensed. No subscription, no usage caps, no billing. Yours to use and modify forever.

Model support

## Local first. Cloud when you need it.

Start private and offline. Reach for the cloud only when a task demands it.

results.title: Introducing Osaurus - native Mac app for any AI provider, local or cloud
results.content: Osaurus is a sleek, native Mac app that serves as your unified gateway to AI, whether you're running models locally on Apple Silicon or connecting to cloud providers. Built specifically for macOS with Swift and optimized using Apple's MLX framework, it delivers fast, privacy-focused performance without the bloat of cross-platform tools. You can download and run popular open-source models like Llama, Qwen, Gemma, or Mistral directly on your Mac—no data leaves your device—while seamlessly switching to services like OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, xAI, or OpenRouter whenever you need more power. Your conversations, context, memory, and custom tools stay portable across all providers, giving you true ownership of your AI experience in a lightweight package (around 10-15MB) that's always available

results.title: Osaurus brings both local and cloud AI models to your Mac
results.content:
Sarah Perez

As AI models increasingly become commoditized, startups are racing to build the software layer that sits on top of them. One interesting entrant into this space is Osaurus, an open source, Apple-only LLM server that lets users move between different local AI models, either locally or in the cloud, while keeping their files and tools all on their own hardware.

Osaurus evolved out of the idea for a desktop AI companion, Dinoki, which Osaurus co-founder Terence Pae described as a sort of “AI-powered Clippy.” Dinoki’s customers had asked him why they should buy the app if they still had to pay for tokens — the usage units AI companies charge for processing prompts and generating responses.

That got Pae thinking more deeply about running AI locally. [...] “That’s how Osaurus started,” Pae, previously a software engineer at Tesla and Netflix, told TechCrunch over a call. The idea, he explained, was to try to run an AI assistant locally. “You can do pretty much everything on your Mac locally, like browsing your files, accessing your browser, accessing your system configurations. I figured this would be a great way to position Osaurus as a personal AI for individuals.”

Pae began building the tool in public as an open source project, adding features and fixing bugs along the way.

Image Credits:Osaurus, Inc. [...] Image Credits:Osaurus, Inc.

Osaurus today can run MiniMax M2.5, Gemma 4, Qwen3.6, GPT-OSS, Llama, DeepSeek V4, and other models. It also supports Apple’s on-device foundation models, Liquid AI’s LFM family of on-device models, and in the cloud, it can connect to OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, xAI/Grok, Venice AI, OpenRouter, Ollama, and LM Studio.

As a full MCP (Model Context Protocol) server, you can give any MCP-compatible client access to your tools as well. Plus, it ships with over 20 native plug-ins for Mail, Calendar, Vision, macOS Use, XLSX, PPTX, Browser, Music, Git, Filesystem, Search, Fetch, and more.

More recently, Osaurus was updated to include voice capabilities as well.
